perturbation.original_text: 3. Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible 1 Transaction and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. Each Party shall not be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the sentence 'Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives' from Section 3 creates a direct contradiction. Previously, Section 3 explicitly held each party accountable for breaches by their representatives. Removing this sentence now implies that parties are not liable for their representatives' actions, which directly contradicts the initial intention of strict confidentiality and accountability outlined in the rest of Section 3. This undermines the enforcement of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Use and Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information provided to it by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. The Receiving Party shall keep the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided , however , that (i) the Receiving Party may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Information provided to it by the Disclosing Party to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party may be disclosed to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who reasonably require access to such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction. Each Party shall not be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the sentence 'Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by any of its Representatives' from Section 3, while the rest of Section 3 describes that the Receiving party should keep the confidential information, and not disclose it. The original text made each party responsible for their representative's breaches, so the removal of this creates an in-text contradiction, as it is no longer clear if parties are liable, creating uncertainty, inconsistency, and unenforceability, and fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Standstill . For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall not, and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party), (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Standstill . For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall, and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party). Notwithstanding the above, acquiring less than 5% ownership of a publicly traded company is acceptable, (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Notwithstanding the above, acquiring less than 5% ownership of a publicly traded company is acceptable' to section 10 introduces an in-text contradiction. The original standstill clause prohibits any acquisition of ownership. The addition creates an exception, allowing less than 5% ownership acquisition, which directly contradicts the absolute prohibition stated earlier in the same section, introducing ambiguity as to the scope of the standstill agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Standstill . For a period of one (1) year from the date of this Agreement, each Party agrees that it shall, and that it shall cause is affiliates and subsidiaries who have received the other Party’s Confidential Information, or who are aware of the possibility of the Transaction, not to, directly or indirectly, unless specifically invited in advance by the other Party’s Board of Directors: (i) acquire or agree, offer, seek or propose to acquire, or sell or otherwise dispose of, ownership (including, but not limited to, beneficial ownership as defined in Rule 13d-3 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) of any of the other Party’s assets or businesses or any securities issued by such other Party, or any rights or options to acquire such ownership (including from a third party). Notwithstanding the above, acquiring less than 5% ownership of a publicly traded company is acceptable, (ii) seek or propose to influence or control of the other Party’s management or policies (or request permission to do so), or (iii) enter into any discussions, negotiations, arrangements or understandings with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 10 establishes a complete standstill, prohibiting any acquisition of ownership. The changed text modifies section 10 by adding an exception that allows acquiring less than 5% ownership of a publicly traded company. This exception directly contradicts the initial absolute prohibition, creating an in-text contradiction as both statements exist within the same section, as stated by the given explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 11. No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation by AZZ . During the course of AZZ’s evaluation of the Transaction, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to employees or representatives of North American specified by North American. Accordingly, AZZ agrees not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any executive or other employee of North American concerning the Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the consent of North American. AZZ also agrees not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in the Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition without the prior written consent of AZZ. For a period of two (2) years after the date hereof, without the prior written consent of North American, neither AZZ nor any of its affiliates, whom AZZ has made aware of this Agreement or to whom AZZ has disclosed Confidential Information, shall, directly or indirectly, in any individual, representative or other capacity, employ or engage, or solicit for employment or engagement, any employee of, or any person whose activities are principally dedicated to, North American whom AZZ is made aware of or who AZZ has direct contact with as a result of AZZ’s consideration of the Transaction or otherwise seek to influence or alter any such person’s relationship with North American; provided , however , that the foregoing shall not prohibit AZZ from: (i) general advertising not directed toward employees of North American or hiring employees responding to such advertising; (ii) hiring any person who initiates discussions with AZZ regarding employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by AZZ or; (iii) hiring any person whose employment with North American has been terminated (by either that employee or North American) prior to and independent of any communication with AZZ or any of AZZ’s representatives or agents regarding employment. If the foregoing provision shall be adjudicated to be invalid or unenforceable, such provision shall be amended to reduce the time period or 3 otherwise amended as is necessary to cause such provision to be valid or enforceable, and such amendment shall apply only with respect to the operation of this provision in the particular jurisdiction in which such adjudication is made.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation by AZZ . During the course of AZZ’s evaluation of the Transaction, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to employees or representatives of North American specified by North American. Accordingly, AZZ agrees to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any executive or other employee of North American concerning the Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the consent of North American. AZZ also agrees not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in the Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition without the prior written consent of AZZ. For a period of two (2) years after the date hereof, without the prior written consent of North American, neither AZZ nor any of its affiliates, whom AZZ has made aware of this Agreement or to whom AZZ has disclosed Confidential Information, shall, directly or indirectly, in any individual, representative or other capacity, employ or engage, or solicit for employment or engagement, any employee of, or any person whose activities are principally dedicated to, North American whom AZZ is made aware of or who AZZ has direct contact with as a result of AZZ’s consideration of the Transaction or otherwise seek to influence or alter any such person’s relationship with North American; provided , however , that the foregoing shall not prohibit AZZ from: (i) general advertising not directed toward employees of North American or hiring employees responding to such advertising; (ii) hiring any person who initiates discussions with AZZ regarding employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by AZZ or; (iii) hiring any person whose employment with North American has been terminated (by either that employee or North American) prior to and independent of any communication with AZZ or any of AZZ’s representatives or agents regarding employment. If the foregoing provision shall be adjudicated to be invalid or unenforceable, such provision shall be amended to reduce the time period or 3 otherwise amended as is necessary to cause such provision to be valid or enforceable, and such amendment shall apply only with respect to the operation of this provision in the particular jurisdiction in which such adjudication is made.
perturbation.explanation: In section 11, changing 'AZZ agrees not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate' to 'AZZ agrees to directly or indirectly contact or communicate' creates a direct contradiction. The original intent was to prevent unauthorized contact, but the modified text permits and even agrees to such contact. This is contradictory to the purpose and initial statement of the 'No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation by AZZ . During the course of AZZ’s evaluation of the Transaction, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to employees or representatives of North American specified by North American. Accordingly, AZZ agrees to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any executive or other employee of North American concerning the Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the consent of North American. AZZ also agrees not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in the Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition without the prior written consent of AZZ. For a period of two (2) years after the date hereof, without the prior written consent of North American, neither AZZ nor any of its affiliates, whom AZZ has made aware of this Agreement or to whom AZZ has disclosed Confidential Information, shall, directly or indirectly, in any individual, representative or other capacity, employ or engage, or solicit for employment or engagement, any employee of, or any person whose activities are principally dedicated to, North American whom AZZ is made aware of or who AZZ has direct contact with as a result of AZZ’s consideration of the Transaction or otherwise seek to influence or alter any such person’s relationship with North American; provided , however , that the foregoing shall not prohibit AZZ from: (i) general advertising not directed toward employees of North American or hiring employees responding to such advertising; (ii) hiring any person who initiates discussions with AZZ regarding employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by AZZ or; (iii) hiring any person whose employment with North American has been terminated (by either that employee or North American) prior to and independent of any communication with AZZ or any of AZZ’s representatives or agents regarding employment. If the foregoing provision shall be adjudicated to be invalid or unenforceable, such provision shall be amended to reduce the time period or 3 otherwise amended as is necessary to cause such provision to be valid or enforceable, and such amendment shall apply only with respect to the operation of this provision in the particular jurisdiction in which such adjudication is made.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11 modifies 'AZZ agrees not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate' to 'AZZ agrees to directly or indirectly contact or communicate'. This creates a direct and strong contradiction with the section's title, 'No Unauthorized Contact or Solicitation by AZZ,' and the initial intent to prevent such contact. The modified text now permits and even agrees to unauthorized contact, which directly conflicts with the contradicted text also being Section 11, thereby creating an in-text contradiction under the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as a clause is placed in the same location but has conflicting implications.
